title: dinotrust audit event
description: One JSONL line per flagged inbound. Emitted identically by every producer: code-hook adapters (Tier 1/2) and the agent self-audit clause (Tier 3). The digest reads this shape regardless of which platform or tier produced it. rule_id values MUST exist in security_rules.md.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC of the inbound message.

senderId.description: Platform-injected authoritative sender id (Telegram from.id, Discord author.id, etc.). Never username/display name.

senderName.description: Display name, for human-readable reports only. NOT authoritative.

conversationId.description: Conversation/chat/thread id where the attempt occurred.

messageId.description: Platform message id of the flagged inbound.

rule_ids.description: Distinct rule_ids triggered, from security_rules.md (e.g. R1_override_claims, S0_security_directive).

patterns.description: Convenience list of pattern ids (= hits[].id). Optional, for quick scanning.

content.description: Raw inbound text. Privacy-controlled: producers SHOULD honor the install --privacy level (patterns-only omits this, truncated caps length, full keeps it).

producer.description: Which tier produced this line. agent-self-audit (Tier 3) is best-effort and may be suppressed by a successful jailbreak.
